title: Vignette: Using the Count Regression Toolkit
text: This vignette demonstrates how to use the Shiny App to fit and evaluate count regression models.

title: Vignette: Using the Count Regression Toolkit
text: This vignette demonstrates how to use the Shiny App to fit and evaluate count regression models.

title: Vignette: Using the Count Regression Toolkit
text:
Step 1: Load Data
Open the Shiny App and upload a CSV file with a count response variable.

title: Vignette: Using the Count Regression Toolkit
text:
Step 2: Enter a Model Formula
Use a formula such as:
count_response ~ predictor1 + predictor2

title: Vignette: Using the Count Regression Toolkit
text:
Step 3: Fit a Regression
Click Fit Model.

title: Vignette: Using the Count Regression Toolkit
Count Regression Toolkit
text: This website documents my Data Analysis II software toolkit for count regression.

title: Count Regression Toolkit

title: Count Regression Toolkit
text:
Launching the App
The Shiny app is included in this GitHub repository (https://dannymolyneux.github.io/).
To run the app:

Clone the repository from GitHub: https://github.com/dannymolyneux/dannymolyneux.github.io
Open R or RStudio
Run:

shiny::runApp()
Or open the “app.r” file, and click “Run”.

title: Count Regression Toolkit
text:
Pages
Use the navigation bar to read about the project and view the vignette.

title: About
text:
This project is a statistical software toolkit designed to automate count regression analysis using an interactive Shiny dashboard.
The toolkit allows users to upload data, specify regression models, and perform count regressions including model fitting/comparisons, condition checks, and interpretation.
